content.subtitle: Form
content.formula: if + past perfect simple, would + have + past participle

content.subtitle: Use
rows: To talk about imaginary past situations — things that did not happen — and to say what the result would have been
If I **had remembered** her birthday, she **would have been** happier.
If they **hadn't missed** the flight, they **would have arrived** on time.
If we **had brought** more food, everyone **would have eaten** enough.
If he **had studied** harder, he **wouldn't have failed** the course.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: We can use **could** or **might** instead of **would** to express different degrees of certainty.
items: If you had trained more, you **might have won** the race. (uncertain outcome)
If she had borrowed my laptop, she **could have finished** the project earlier. (ability in the past)

content.subtitle: Be careful
items: The third conditional always refers to the past: **If I had brought my charger, my phone wouldn't have died.**
The second conditional uses past simple but refers to the present or future: **If I had more free time, I would travel more.**
Second conditional form: **if + past simple, would + bare infinitive** — used for imaginary or unlikely situations in the present or future: **If she lived nearby, we would meet more often.**

sections.title: A: Complete the third conditional sentences
sections.instructions: Use the correct form of the verbs in brackets.

items.sentence: If I ...... (remember) his birthday, I ...... (send) him a card.
If they ...... (book) earlier, they ...... (find) cheaper tickets.
If we ...... (not forget) the camera, we ...... (take) more photos.
If she ...... (listen) to the directions, she ...... (not get) lost.
If he ...... (wear) warmer clothes, he ...... (not catch) a cold.
If you ...... (save) your work, you ...... (not lose) everything.

sections.title: B: Choose would have, could have or might have
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with the best option.

items.sentence: If I had seen your message earlier, I ...... replied immediately.
If we had brought a map, we ...... avoided getting lost.
If she had gone to the doctor sooner, she ...... recovered faster.
If they had left before rush hour, they ...... arrived on time.
If I had borrowed your notes, I ...... studied more effectively.
If he had accepted the invitation, he ...... enjoyed the party.

sections.title: C: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake. Write the correct form.

items.sentence: If I would had more time, I would have helped you.
items.answer: If I had had more time, I would have helped you.

items.sentence: If she had drank less coffee, she would have slept better.
items.answer: If she had drunk less coffee, she would have slept better.

items.sentence: If we had took a taxi, we would have arrived earlier.
items.answer: If we had taken a taxi, we would have arrived earlier.

items.sentence: If they hadn't lose the address, they could have found the house.
items.answer: If they hadn't lost the address, they could have found the house.

items.sentence: If he had trained harder, he would won the competition.
items.answer: If he had trained harder, he would have won the competition.

items.sentence: If you had reminded me, I might helped you.
items.answer: If you had reminded me, I might have helped you.

sections.title: D: Rewrite with the third conditional
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning.

items.sentence:
I forgot my passport, so I couldn't board the plane. **IF**
If I ...... my passport, I could have boarded the plane.

She didn't charge her phone, so it turned off. **HAD**
If she ...... her phone, it wouldn't have turned off.

We didn't reserve a table, so we didn't get a seat. **WOULD**
If we had reserved a table, we ...... a seat.

Tom didn't revise enough, so he failed the test. **COULD**
If Tom had revised more, he ...... the test.

It snowed heavily, so the match was cancelled. **PLAYED**
If it hadn't snowed heavily, they ...... the match.

I stayed up too late, so I felt exhausted. **FELT**
If I hadn't stayed up so late, I ...... exhausted.
